title: Auriculardisia tilaranensis
taxonomy: Auriculardisia tilaranensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is native to Costa Rica and is a member of the genus Auriculardisia. The plant is further classified into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. The order is Ericales and the family is Sapotaceae. Its classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics.

intro: Auriculardisia tilaranensis is a species of plant in the Lauraceae family, native to Central America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Auriculardisia tilaranensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
